subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 12
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which animal is the fastest land creature?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of reaching speeds up to 70 miles per hour.
questions.hint: It has spots and is a big cat.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their traits.

questions.q: What is the capital of Italy?
questions.explanation: Rome is the capital city of Italy and is famous for its ancient history.
questions.hint: It is often called the 'Eternal City'.

questions.q: Which of these is a gas that we breathe out?
questions.explanation: Humans breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide as a waste product.
questions.hint: Plants need this to perform photosynthesis.

questions.q: What is the name of the galaxy we live in?
questions.explanation: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System.
questions.hint: It sounds like a popular chocolate bar.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your teeth?
questions.explanation: Brushing and flossing your teeth helps remove plaque and prevent cavities.
questions.hint: It is something you should do in the morning and before bed.

questions.q: What is the main ingredient in bread?
questions.explanation: Flour is the primary ingredient used to make bread dough.
questions.hint: It is a fine powder usually made from wheat.

questions.q: What is the largest desert in the world?
questions.explanation: While many think of deserts as hot and sandy, a desert is actually defined by how little rain it receives. Antarctica is the largest desert on Earth.
questions.hint: It is at the South Pole.

questions.q: Which of these is a community helper who puts out fires?
questions.explanation: Firefighters are trained to fight fires and perform rescues in emergencies.
questions.hint: They ride in big red trucks.

questions.q: What planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is known as the Red Planet because of the iron oxide on its surface.
questions.hint: It is named after the Roman god of war.
questions.objective: Understand facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which state of matter is rigid and holds its own shape?
questions.explanation: Solids have a definite shape and volume.
questions.hint: It is not a liquid or a gas.
